pre_registered.primary_metric: held-out Sample-II analysis timing sigma68 of predicted clean CFD20 time after injected dropout, in ns
secondary_metrics: abstention coverage of a recover-vs-veto phase policy
pre_registered.split: train on Sample-I runs, calibrate scalar offsets on run 64, evaluate only Sample-II analysis runs 58-63 and 65; confidence intervals resample held-out runs
pre_registered.traditional_method: case-wise train-selected stronger of local interpolation and amplitude/stave/peak-binned template refit
pre_registered.win_rule: winner is the eligible method with lowest held-out sigma68; ML adoption requires its run-bootstrap delta versus traditional to have 95% CI below zero

candidate_next_ticket.title: P06f consumer-specific dropout veto utility
candidate_next_ticket.body: Question: do P06e recoverable/unrecoverable phase bands improve downstream timing, charge, and pile-up consumers when the action is recover versus veto rather than always-correct? Expected information gain: converts the dropout-phase frontier into a consumer-specific decision rule and tests whether a phase-gated CNN correction harms any downstream metric under run-held-out bootstrap CIs.
